todoHits.text: - Add deeper tests for activity creation, join/leave, waiting list, private pending requests, organizer approvals, and edit permissions.
- Chat RLS design: only organizer, confirmed participants, admin, and moderator can read chat; guests, pending users, rejected users, and blocked users cannot.
- SRC-003 Add manual moderation queue for `pending_review`.
- guests, pending users, rejected users, and blocked users cannot read chat.
- pending Activity requests do not create chat membership.
- Invite: pending request until organizer approval.
- Do not notify guests, pending users, rejected users, blocked users, or users who left the chat.
- Guests, pending users, rejected users, and blocked users cannot see the chat.
- pending users do not see private chat;
- Pending users are not chat members.
- Pending Activity membership is not enough for chat membership.
2. User sends request or joins, depending on privacy.
- Participants list with joined, waiting, and pending states
- Private events create pending requests that the organizer can approve or reject.

riskItems: Unlocalized technical/English labels are still present.
window.alert is used for UX feedback. Replace with in-app toast later.
Demo mode exists. Verify it cannot affect production writes.
nextSteps: Stabilize green baseline: lint/build/test must pass before each push.
Finish UX cleanup in event sheet: compact buttons, readable 2-column details, no embedded map junk.
Finish localization: remove pending, Skill match, Indoor/Outdoor, Casual/Outdoor leaks in RU/UK/CS.
Replace bug-report copy/alert MVP with in-app feedback form and Supabase feedback table.
Fix share properly with a dedicated /join/:id landing page and Open Graph image instead of t.me/share hacks.
Verify chat expiration from event date + time + 24h in database and UI.
Add component/e2e tests for create event, join, share, chat, coach request, delete.
Add moderation/admin screen: reports, delete/hide, user/event review.
Prepare private beta checklist: onboarding, city/language defaults, privacy, support flow.
perspective: GO IRL has a clear niche: turning local Telegram communities into real-world micro-events.
The strongest wedge is sport and low-friction meetups, because users immediately understand join/capacity/place/time.
The Telegram Mini App distribution is a real advantage, but it also makes sharing/auth/cache messy. Build robust fallbacks.
The product should avoid becoming a generic event app. Focus on small trusted activities, friends-of-friends, and city communities.
Near-term growth loop: create event -> share to chat -> people join in Telegram -> participants chat -> repeat.
Main risk: UX bugs around auth/share/time will destroy trust faster than missing features. Polish beats feature spam now.
